summary: The plan was written against an imagined API surface that diverges significantly from what shipped in Phase 1A, 1B, 5a, and 5b. Twelve blocking issues were found: four are silent-corruption traps (schema epoch, verbatim service block, two wrong API symbols in test spies), four are definite runtime errors (non-existent module import, wrong component path, wrong component props, unimplemented endpoints), and four are systematic naming errors that will cause every affected file to fail to compile. Multiple major issues include wrong config path (env var mismatch), wrong Playwright spec location, cache path inconsistency, Zustand anti-pattern, and a Pydantic config mismatch. The plan is not safe to execute as written.

blocking.title: SESSION_SCHEMA_EPOCH never bumped — startup crash on existing DBs
blocking.description: Task 1 adds a `tutorial_completed_at` column to `user_preferences_table` but never increments `SESSION_SCHEMA_EPOCH` from 4 to 5. The live `_assert_schema_version()` in `schema.py` checks `PRAGMA user_version` against `SESSION_SCHEMA_EPOCH` and aborts startup with 'Pre-release ELSPETH...' on any mismatch. Any deployment upgrading an existing database will crash at startup. The plan must co-land `SESSION_SCHEMA_EPOCH = 5` in the same commit as the column addition.

blocking.title: Task 3 verbatim block destroys five production safety mechanisms in preferences service

mechanisms_lost: Panel C2 guard: payload_is_empty → no INSERT on empty PATCH against no-row user
wrote_row: bool tracking to distinguish INSERT vs UPDATE
CorruptPreferencesError(user_id, mode) instead of bare RuntimeError

blocking.description: Task 3 Step 3 contains a complete verbatim replacement of `update_composer_preferences` that omits all five of the above production-quality mechanisms. If executed as written, it drops audit-quality corruption detection (`CorruptPreferencesError`) back to bare `RuntimeError`, removes the OTel telemetry, removes the Panel C2 guard that prevents spurious empty-PATCH INSERTs, and removes the `wrote_row` tracking used for conditional response shaping. The plan must be restructured to extend the function rather than replace it.

blocking.title: Task 3 Pydantic model config mismatch — plan uses frozen=True, live model uses strict=True

evidence.live_field: updated_at: datetime | None  # nullable per Panel U1

blocking.description: The `ComposerPreferences` Pydantic model at `src/elspeth/web/preferences/models.py` uses `ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid')`, not `ConfigDict(frozen=True)`. The plan also shows `updated_at: datetime` as non-nullable, but the live model has `updated_at: datetime | None` per Panel U1 contract. The plan's model snippets are incorrect and will produce validation failures or wrong type annotations.

blocking.title: POST /api/tutorial/run and GET .../audit-story are specified but implemented in no task
blocking.description: The backend plan overview section specifies two new API endpoints. No task in the backend plan (Tasks 1–7) actually implements either of them. Tasks 5–7 reference them in prose but do not include route definitions, Pydantic request/response models, service methods, or test coverage. These endpoints are required by frontend Tasks 8 and 9. If the backend plan is executed as written, the frontend will call routes that do not exist.

blocking.title: api/interpretationEvents.ts module does not exist — Task 6 import will fail at build time
blocking.description: The plan imports from `../../api/interpretationEvents` but this module does not exist. Phase 5b shipped interpretation event logic inside `interpretationEventsStore` (a Zustand store), not as a standalone API module. The plan must reference the correct store action surfaces or the `useInterpretationResolver` hook at `src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/hooks/useInterpretationResolver.ts`.

blocking.title: InterpretationReviewTurn path wrong — plan imports from non-existent location
blocking.description: The plan imports `InterpretationReviewTurn` from `../interpretation/InterpretationReviewTurn`. The component lives at `components/chat/guided/InterpretationReviewTurn.tsx`. There is no `components/interpretation/` directory. The import will fail at build time.

blocking.title: InterpretationReviewTurn props mismatch — plan uses {sessionId, interpretationEventId, onResolved}

evidence.live_props: { event: InterpretationEvent, sessionId: string, onResolved?: (newState: CompositionState | null) => void }
evidence.plan_props: { sessionId: string, interpretationEventId: string, onResolved: () => void }

blocking.description: The live component requires the full `InterpretationEvent` object (not an ID string), `sessionId`, and an optional `onResolved` callback with signature `(newState: CompositionState | null) => void`. The plan passes `interpretationEventId: string` (an ID) instead of the event object, and uses `onResolved: () => void` (no argument). The component will not compile with the plan's JSX.

blocking.title: ComposerPreferences frontend type collision — plan extends per-session type with tutorial_completed_at

evidence.collision: ComposerPreferences in src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/types/api.ts line 17 is the PER-SESSION type (trust_mode / density_default)
evidence.field_to_add: tutorial_completed_at should go on UserComposerPreferencesPayload, not ComposerPreferences

blocking.description: `ComposerPreferences` in `types/api.ts` is the per-session type for `trust_mode` and `density_default`. The user-account preferences type is `UserComposerPreferencesPayload`. The plan adds `tutorial_completed_at` to `ComposerPreferences`, which will corrupt the per-session type with an unrelated user-account field. All consumers of `ComposerPreferences` for per-session state will pick up this phantom field. The field belongs on `UserComposerPreferencesPayload`.

blocking.title: Plan test spies reference getComposerPreferences / updateComposerPreferences — wrong function names

evidence.note: updateComposerPreferences EXISTS in client.ts but operates on per-session prefs (trust_mode/density_default), not user-account prefs

blocking.description: The test spies name functions that do not match live API client exports. `getComposerPreferences` does not exist; the user-account preferences fetch is `fetchUserComposerPreferences`. Using `updateComposerPreferences` would spy on the per-session preferences update, not the user-account update. Tests will silently spy on the wrong function or fail with 'property does not exist'.

blocking.title: setBannerDismissed does not exist — live name is dismissDefaultChangedBanner
blocking.description: The plan's recon checklist and likely generated code expect `setBannerDismissed` on the preferences store. The live action is `dismissDefaultChangedBanner`. Any generated code, test spy, or store reference using `setBannerDismissed` will fail to compile.

blocking.title: runTutorialPipeline and getRunAuditSummary missing from client.ts — test spies will fail
blocking.description: The frontend tests for Turn 4 (run pipeline) and Turn 5 (audit story) spy on `runTutorialPipeline` and `getRunAuditSummary` in `api/client.ts`. Neither function exists. These must be added to `client.ts` as part of Task 8/9 implementation, but the plan never instructs their creation in the client module.

blocking.title: Zustand class-style getter get tutorialCompleted() is an invalid pattern

evidence.zustand_pattern: Zustand stores use create() factory with plain object — no class instances, no getter syntax
evidence.live_example: src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/stores/preferencesStore.ts — all derived values are computed inline in selectors or in component hooks

blocking.description: Zustand's `create()` factory produces a plain store object, not a class instance. ES5 `get name()` getter syntax is invalid in this context and will cause a runtime error or TypeScript compilation failure. Derived boolean state like `tutorialCompleted` must be computed via a selector function passed to the `useStore()` hook, not declared as a getter on the store object.

major.title: renameSession missing from client.ts — Task 10 test spy will fail
major.description: `renameSession` does not exist in `api/client.ts`. The plan must add this function before the Turn 6 test can compile. Like `runTutorialPipeline` and `getRunAuditSummary`, its creation is implicit but never explicitly tasked.

major.title: Tutorial cache path inconsistent across three locations in backend plan

evidence.live_config: WebSettings.data_dir = Path('data') with env prefix ELSPETH_WEB__DATA_DIR

major.description: Three different path specifications appear: Task 5 uses a hardcoded fallback to `/var/lib/elspeth/` with the env var `ELSPETH_DATA_DIR`, Task 6 references `settings.tutorial_cache_dir` (a field that does not exist in `WebSettings`), and the live `WebSettings` has `data_dir: Path = Field(default=Path('data'))` under the `ELSPETH_WEB__` prefix. The plan must pick one approach, use `WebSettings.data_dir / 'tutorial_cache'`, and add `tutorial_cache_dir` to `WebSettings` with the correct env var prefix.

major.title: Playwright E2E spec at wrong path — project root vs frontend tests directory
major.description: Playwright's `testDir` in `playwright.config.ts` is `./tests/e2e` relative to the frontend package root, resolving to `src/elspeth/web/frontend/tests/e2e/`. The plan places the spec at `tests/e2e/tutorial.e2e.spec.ts` from the project root, which Playwright will never discover. The correct path is `src/elspeth/web/frontend/tests/e2e/tutorial.spec.ts` using the `.spec.ts` extension convention matched by all existing specs.

major.title: preferencesStore bootstrap action called loadPreferences in plan prose, not bootstrap
major.description: The plan refers to `loadPreferences()` as the store's bootstrap action. The live method is named `bootstrap()`. Any generated code using `loadPreferences()` will fail to compile.

major.title: Tutorial component directory does not exist — parent path must be created
major.description: The plan places tutorial components at `src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/components/tutorial/`. This directory does not yet exist. This is correctly marked as 'to be created' implicitly, but the plan should make the directory creation explicit so implementing agents don't assume it exists.

minor.title: tutorial_completed_at nullable in plan vs. plan prose implies non-nullable
minor.description: The plan alternately describes `tutorial_completed_at` as nullable (SQL column allows NULL) and as a required field. The correct design is `TIMESTAMP NULLABLE` (NULL = tutorial not completed). The Pydantic model should reflect `tutorial_completed_at: datetime | None`.

minor.title: optedOutAtSessionId watermark not mentioned — tutorial turn 6 must not reset it
minor.description: The preferences store carries an `optedOutAtSessionId` watermark used by `DefaultModeChangedBanner` to suppress the banner within the current session after a freeform opt-out. The plan's `markTutorialCompleted` action must not reset or disturb this field. The plan does not mention it, which creates risk that a naive implementation clears it.

minor.title: writing guard must be respected by markTutorialCompleted action
minor.description: Both `setDefaultMode` and `dismissDefaultChangedBanner` gate on `if (get().writing) return` to serialise concurrent PATCH calls. The new `markTutorialCompleted` action must obey the same gate, or a concurrent call could corrupt the optimistic state.

minor.title: UpdateUserComposerPreferencesPayload missing tutorial_completed_at

evidence.live_interface: UpdateUserComposerPreferencesPayload { default_mode?: ComposerMode; banner_dismissed_at?: string | null; }

minor.description: The PATCH payload type `UpdateUserComposerPreferencesPayload` in `types/api.ts` and `UpdateComposerPreferencesRequest` in Python must both gain an optional `tutorial_completed_at` field. The plan adds the column but does not explicitly update these request payload types.

verified_correct.item: Phase 5a inline_blob bias in composer skill is accurate
verified_correct.evidence: src/elspeth/web/composer/skills/pipeline_composer.md contains 'Default to inline_blob when the user types data into chat' rule
src/elspeth/web/sessions/models.py line 460 — accepted_value Text nullable=True confirmed

verified_correct.item: source_data_hash exists in Landscape core schema
user_preferences_table schema matches plan's read surface (sans tutorial_completed_at)
verified_correct.evidence: src/elspeth/web/sessions/models.py — user_id, default_composer_mode, banner_dismissed_at, updated_at confirmed

verified_correct.item: preferencesStore has dismissDefaultChangedBanner, writing guard, optimistic-write + revert
Playwright is set up for the frontend package
useInterpretationResolver hook exists (plan can reference it)
ConfirmDialog component exists at expected path
CorruptPreferencesError is a named subclass (plan must preserve it)
